event1.title: Risk 1
event1.summary: This is a major security risk.

event2.title: Risk 2
event2.summary: This is a moderate security risk.

event3.title: Risk 3
event3.summary: This is a minor security risk.

United States.title: US Supreme Court Strikes Down Affirmative Action in College Admissions
United States.summary: The US Supreme Court ruled that affirmative action policies in college admissions are unconstitutional, sparking widespread protests and concerns about the impact on marginalized communities.

United States.title: Tensions Rise Between US and Russia Over Ukraine War
United States.summary: The US and Russia have raised their rhetoric over the Ukraine war, with the US imposing new sanctions on Russia and Russia threatening to respond with military force.

United States.title: Twitter Hacker Arrested for Breach of Security
United States.summary: A hacker was arrested for breaching Twitter's security and accessing the private data of several high-profile users.

South Korea.title: North Korea tests new rocket engine
South Korea.summary: South Korea's military is on high alert after North Korea tested a new rocket engine, sparking concerns of a possible provocation.

South Korea.title: Protests erupt in South Korea over military conscription
South Korea.summary: Large-scale protests broke out in South Korea over the government's plan to reintroduce military conscription, with demonstrators calling for the policy to be scrapped.

South Korea.title: Hackers target South Korean government websites
South Korea.summary: South Korean government websites were targeted by hackers, highlighting concerns over the country's cybersecurity and potential attacks from North Korea.

Japan.summary: A 6.0-magnitude earthquake struck off the coast of Japan's northern island of Hokkaido on June 24, but no tsunami warning was issued. Japan's Meteorological Agency said the earthquake occurred at 9:22 a.m. local time and its epicenter was at a depth of about 300 kilometers. The Pacific Tsunami Warning Center said there was no threat of a tsunami from the earthquake.

A Japanese defense ministry report showed that the country's defense spending was 1.07% of its GDP, exceeding the 1.06% target, in fiscal 2022. Japan's defense spending has been under pressure to increase since the 2013 Guidelines for U.S.-Japan Defense Cooperation and the 2022 National Security Strategy were released.

Russia's Deputy Foreign Minister has accused Japan of not being willing to hold talks on a World War 2 peace treaty, following a visit to Japan by Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi. The Japanese side has not made any formal response to Russia's accusations on the peace treaty talks yet.
